name: Pearl City
desc: Central Oahu's workhorse suburb — Pearlridge Mall, diverse community, easy freeway access, solid and unpretentious.
insider: Pearlridge Mall has two wings (Downtown and Uptown) connected by a monorail — the only shopping mall monorail in Hawaii
Pearl City has some of the best hole-in-the-wall Asian food on the island — Gyotaku for sushi, Chun Wah Kam for noodles, and Tsukuneya for yakitori
The Pearl City community garden program along the bike path is active and welcoming to newcomers — plots are affordable
Freeway on-ramp access is excellent — you can be downtown in 20 minutes or at the North Shore in 30 on a good day
The Pearl City bike path runs along the old Oahu Railway route and connects to Waipahu — flat and scenic for family rides
Watercress grows wild in Pearl City's streams — old-timers still harvest it, and the area was historically famous for watercress farms
Don Quijote (formerly Daiei) on Kamehameha Highway is open 24 hours and has the best selection of Japanese snacks and bento outside Honolulu
Pearl City Highlands at the upper end has better views and cooler breezes but steeper streets — the trade-off is worth it
Anna Miller's was a legendary 24-hour pie restaurant here for decades — old-timers still mourn its closure
The Pearl Harbor bike path extension lets you ride all the way to the Arizona Memorial visitor center — a gorgeous and moving ride
